news.headline: US bank deposits rose to $17.865 tln from $17.806 tln in prior week.

New study in ADLM's The Journal of Applied Laboratory Medicine shows a streamlined approach to testing for heparin-induced thrombocytopenia, improving diagnostic accuracy. Published in a special issue, the findings aim to advance healthcare value. Washington, Jan. 3, 2025.
news.summary: Research results were recently released in a dedicated edition of ADLM's publication, The Journal of Applied Laboratory Medicine, with the theme "Diagnostic stewardship in action: Advancing healthcare value." The study, conducted by the Association for Diagnostics & Laboratory Medicine (ADLM), was published on January 3, 2025, in Washington. This study presents valuable insights into healthcare practices to enhance efficiency and value. It offers crucial information on diagnostic stewardship, contributing to the advancement of the healthcare sector. The report sheds light on key aspects that are essential for improving healthcare outcomes and overall effectiveness.

news.headline: Special issue discussing diagnostic stewardship in healthcare collaboration released by ADLM's The Journal of Applied Laboratory Medicine.
news.summary: On January 3, 2025, a report highlighted the significance of diagnostic stewardship in healthcare, emphasizing the importance of healthcare professionals collaborating to ensure patients receive appropriate clinical laboratory tests promptly. The report was issued by the Association for Diagnostics & Laboratory Medicine (ADLM). This collaboration aims to facilitate the delivery of accurate and timely medical assessments, improving patient care outcomes.

news.headline: CNX Resources responds to final rules for Section 45V Hydrogen Production Tax Credit; Dept of Treasury's recognition noted.
news.summary: On January 3, 2025, CNX Resources Corporation (NYSE: CNX) issued a statement in Pittsburgh regarding the final rules of the Inflation Reduction Act's Section 45V Hydrogen Production Tax Credit. The Department of Treasury's recent actions were acknowledged by CNX Resources Corporation in response to the regulation.

news.headline: Automakers end 2024 on a high note as E.V. demand rises. General Motors leads U.S. sales, increasing E.V. sales by over 100%.
news.summary: In the final quarter of the year, General Motors emerged as the top performer in U.S. sales by achieving a significant increase of 21%. Of note, the company saw a remarkable surge in its electric vehicle sales, more than doubling compared to previous periods.

Tandy Leather Factory names new CEO, Johan Hedberg, to replace Janet Carr; effective January 6.
news.summary: Fort Worth, Texas, on January 3, 2025 - Tandy Leather Factory, Inc. (Nasdaq: TLF) has named Johan Hedberg as its latest Chief Executive Officer and Board of Directors member, starting from Monday, January 6. The appointment of Mr. Hedberg follows the tenure of Janet Carr, who will continue to support the Company during the transition period until March 2025.

Portland's new city councilors elect Elana Pirtle-Guiney as leader after 2-year government transition.
news.summary: In Oregon's Portland, on January 3, 2025, the newly elected officials made their debut at City Hall's council chambers for their inaugural meeting as the city's legislative body. This marked the launch of Portland's revamped government structure, with councilors choosing Elana Pirtle-Guiney as council member.

The agreement comes in the form of a term sheet and demonstrates LPOXY's commitment to expanding its portfolio. This strategic move aims to bolster LPOXY's capabilities in infectious disease prevention.

news.headline: IAS announces CFO transition, Jill Putman appointed interim CFO as Tania Secor departs. Jan. 3, 2025. ($IAS)
news.summary: In New York on January 3, 2025, Integral Ad Science (Nasdaq: IAS), a prominent global media measurement and optimization platform, revealed that Jill Putman has been named as the Interim CFO following the departure of Tania Secor. Putman also reaffirmed the company's Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 outlook.

news.headline: Wall Street rebounds after five-day slump, S&P 500 sectors gain, Nasdaq 100 leads charge, US Steel tumbles. Treasury yields hit 4.80%, oil settles at $73.82 per barrel.
news.summary: Stocks on Wall Street bounced back strongly on Friday, possibly ending a recent five-day decline, which was the lengthiest since April. Investors reentered the market with newfound confidence, causing major indices to all trend upwards, led by the Nasdaq 100. Each of the S&P 500 sectors saw gains, showcasing widespread market strength. The ISM Manufacturing PMI for December surpassed expectations, indicating a healthier manufacturing sector for the second consecutive month. Treasury yields also rose, with the 30-year yield climbing to 4.80%. Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin hinted at a more hawkish approach in his speech, emphasizing the importance of maintaining tight monetary policy to combat inflation risks. Oil prices rose by over 1% to settle at $73.82 per barrel, extending their streak of six consecutive days of increases. Contrastingly, natural gas prices dropped by almost 8%.

news.summary: Miami, January 3, 2025 - PennantPark Investment Corporation (NYSE: PNNT) has announced a monthly distribution of $0.08 per share for January 2025. The payment is set to be made on February 3, 2025, to stockholders recorded as of January 15, 2025. This distribution is anticipated to derive from taxable net investment income. Further details regarding the tax attributes of the distribution will be disclosed to stockholders via Form 1099 post the calendar year's conclusion and in the Company's routine submission to the Securities and Exchange Commission.

NETCLASS TECHNOLOGY INC's underwriters exercised full Over-Allotment Option, purchasing 270,000 shares at $5/share, raising $1.35M. Total shares sold in IPO now 2,070,000, yielding $10.35M. Trading as "NTCL" on Nasdaq Capital Market since Dec 13, 2024.
news.summary: On January 3, 2025, NETCLASS TECHNOLOGY INC, a prominent B2B smart education IT solutions provider headquartered in Shanghai, Hong Kong, and Singapore, disclosed that the underwriters of its recent initial public offering (IPO) have fully exercised their over-allotment option. This resulted in the purchase of an additional 270,000 Class A ordinary shares at $5.00 per share, generating extra gross proceeds amounting to $1.35 million. Consequently, the total Class A ordinary shares sold in the Offering increased to 2,070,000, raising the gross proceeds to $10.35 million, exclusive of underwriting discounts and associated expenses. The closing date for the Over-Allotment Option was on the same day, while trading for the Company's stock under the symbol “NTCL” on the Nasdaq Capital Market had commenced on December 13, 2024.

news.headline: Fate Therapeutics grants 72,000 stock options and 3,200 RSUs to new employees, subject to four-year vesting terms.
news.summary: Fate Therapeutics, Inc. granted non-qualified stock options to two new non-executive employees to purchase a total of 72,000 shares at $1.79 per share, based on the closing price on January 2, 2025. The company also granted restricted stock units (RSUs) representing 3,200 shares to another new non-executive employee. The options and RSUs were approved by the Compensation Committee and will vest over four years, with 25% vesting on the one-year anniversary and the remaining vesting in equal monthly installments over the next three years, contingent on continued employment. This move was made under the Company’s Amended and Restated Inducement Equity Plan in line with Nasdaq rules.

Sono Group N.V. to implement reverse share split on Jan 6, 2025, sign SPA for $5M debenture, exchange all debt to equity. Strengthening financials for Nasdaq uplisting.
news.summary: Recent actions have been taken to improve the financial standing of the company, which is crucial in preparations for potential uplisting to Nasdaq. These efforts include successfully securing $5 million in funding through strategic partnerships and bolstering cash reserves by $3 million. These developments signify a positive shift towards meeting the requirements for Nasdaq uplisting and demonstrate a commitment to enhancing the company's overall financial health.

news.summary: On January 3, 2025, Cytokinetics, Incorporated (Nasdaq: CYTK) reported granting stock options for 52,188 shares, 33,885 restricted stock units (RSUs), and 3,189 performance stock units (PSUs) to 9 new employees hired in December 2024. These stock options are a material inducement to their employment and will be settled in shares of common stock upon vesting.

news.summary: As per the outlined strategy, specified FTX platform users with claims amounting to under $50,000 might anticipate the return of their funds within a span of 60 days.

news.headline: Genetic testing could enhance treatment for patients with Candida auris. Study in Clinical Chemistry suggests personalized approach.
news.summary: A recent study featured in the special Clinical Chemistry edition titled "Genomics: Current & emerging trends in the clinical laboratory" revealed groundbreaking results. Published on January 3, 2025, in Washington, the study highlighted the potential of genetic testing to predict the effectiveness of drugs for individual patients. By analyzing genetic markers, researchers identified which medications would be efficacious or ineffective for specific individuals. This advancement opens new possibilities in personalized medicine by offering tailored treatments based on patients' genetic makeup, ultimately enhancing healthcare outcomes.

CPSC advises on preventing carbon monoxide poisoning and fires as winter weather affects millions across the US.
news.summary: Amidst the winter weather affecting a large portion of the nation this week, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) is emphasizing the importance of safeguarding against carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning and fires. With the current conditions, consumers are advised to be cautious when using generators to prevent unfortunate incidents. It is crucial for individuals to prioritize safety measures as part of their preparations.

news.headline: Core Laboratories to host Q4 2024 earnings call at 7:30am CST on Jan 30, 2025. CEO Larry Bruno and team to discuss.
news.summary: On January 30, 2025, Core Laboratories (NYSE: "CLB") is set to conduct its fourth quarter 2024 investor and analyst conference call at 7:30 a.m. CST / 8:30 a.m. EST. The call will feature Larry Bruno, Chairman and CEO, Chris Hill, CFO, and Gwen Gresham, SVP Corporate. This event is significant as it provides stakeholders an insight into the company's performance and strategic outlook. Core Laboratories' financial results and operational updates will be discussed, impacting the trading and investment decisions related to the company's stock. Investors and analysts keen on the company's trajectory and future prospects are anticipated to participate in this call to gain valuable information.

Global online language learning market to grow by USD 81.55 Billion (2025-2029) at 27.5% CAGR, driven by cost benefits and AI.
news.summary: As of January 3, 2025, a recent report highlighting the influence of artificial intelligence on market patterns projects a substantial rise in the global online language learning sector. The market size is expected to increase by a noteworthy USD 81.55 billion between 2025 and 2029, as per data from Technavio. It is anticipated that the market will experience a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 27.5% throughout the forecast period.

news.headline: Base may launch tokenized COIN stock pending regulatory guidance on securities tokenization.
news.summary: Coinbase is currently waiting for regulatory confirmation on securities tokenization, causing uncertainty regarding their future plans. The lack of clarity has put a hold on any concrete decisions, leaving the company in limbo.

news.headline: Claim $20 per Apple device, up to $100 per household, in the $95M Siri privacy settlement for alleged excessive listening.
news.summary: Consumers who owned Apple devices may qualify for a payment of $20 per device, with a maximum compensation of $100 per household. This is due to allegations that Siri, Apple's virtual assistant, may have been eavesdropping on users more than authorized.

news.headline: Mechanical seals market projected to grow by $1.75 billion (2025-2029), aftermarket sales boost revenue, AI reshapes market.
news.summary: A recent report highlights the impact of artificial intelligence on the market, revealing that the global mechanical seals market is projected to expand by a substantial USD 1.75 billion between 2025 and 2029. The research conducted by Technavio forecasts a steady growth trajectory, with the market expected to increase at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) exceeding 5.9% throughout the forecast period.
